name: Deep Research Agent — Telegram → Google Docs

nodes.name: Telegram Trigger
nodes.notes: SETUP: Create a Telegram bot via @BotFather → get the token → add it as a Telegram credential in n8n. Then send any message to your bot to trigger the agent.

parameters.text: ={{ '🔍 Starting research on: *' + $json.topic + '*\n\nThis takes 60–90 seconds. I\'ll send you the Google Docs link when done.' }}

nodes.name: Telegram — Acknowledge
nodes.notes: Sends an immediate reply so the user knows the agent is running.

nodes.name: Claude — Generate search queries
nodes.notes: Agent step 1: Claude breaks the topic into 5 targeted search queries covering different angles.

nodes.name: Split into individual queries
nodes.notes: Creates one item per search query so they can run in parallel.

nodes.name: SerpAPI — Web search
nodes.notes: SETUP: Sign up at serpapi.com — 100 free searches/month is enough for testing. Replace YOUR_SERPAPI_KEY. Alternative: use Brave Search API (also free tier, replace URL with https://api.search.brave.com/res/v1/web/search and add header 'X-Subscription-Token').

assignments.name: search_results
assignments.value: ={{ $json.organic_results ? $json.organic_results.slice(0,3).map(r => ({ title: r.title, url: r.link, snippet: r.snippet })) : [] }}

nodes.name: Merge all search results
nodes.notes: Combines results from all 5 searches into a single item for Claude to analyse.

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
  "max_tokens": 4096,
  "system": "You are an expert research analyst. You write comprehensive, well-structured research reports that are used by executives and decision-makers. Your reports are factual, balanced, and cite sources throughout.

Write in clear professional English. Use the source URLs provided — cite them inline as [Source: URL]. Never invent facts or URLs.",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Write a comprehensive research report on the following topic:

TOPIC: {{ $('Extract topic and chat ID').item.json.topic }}

SOURCE MATERIAL (search results collected from the web):
{{ JSON.stringify($json.data) }}

Write the report with these exact sections, using these exact headings:

# Research Report: {{ $('Extract topic and chat ID').item.json.topic }}

## Executive Summary
[3-4 sentences capturing the most important insight. What does someone need to know if they only read this section?]

## Key Findings
[5-7 bullet points. Each finding must cite its source inline as (Source: URL). Be specific — include numbers, names, dates where available.]

## Pros & Cons
### Advantages
[3-5 bullet points]
### Challenges & Risks
[3-5 bullet points]

## Competitor & People Landscape
[Who are the key players, companies, or people in this space? What are they doing? 3-5 entries with sources.]

## Action Recommendations
[3-5 concrete, specific actions someone could take based on this research. Be direct.]

## Sources
[List all URLs cited in the report as a numbered list]

---
Report generated: {{ new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-GB', {day:'numeric', month:'long', year:'numeric'}) }}"
    }
  ]
}

nodes.name: Claude — Write research report
nodes.notes: Agent step 2: Claude synthesises all search results into a structured 5-section report with citations.

assignments.name: doc_title
assignments.value: ={{ 'Research: ' + $('Extract topic and chat ID').item.json.topic + ' — ' + new Date().toLocaleDateString('en-GB') }}

nodes.name: Google Docs — Create report
nodes.notes: SETUP: Add Google Docs OAuth2 credentials in n8n. Go to Credentials → New → Google Docs OAuth2. You'll need to create a Google Cloud project and enable the Google Docs API — n8n's docs walk through this at docs.n8n.io/integrations/builtin/credentials/google/

parameters.text:
={{ '✅ *Research complete!*

📄 *' + $('Prepare report for Docs').item.json.doc_title + '*

🔗 [Open in Google Docs](https://docs.google.com/document/d/' + $json.id + '/edit)

Sections included:
• Executive summary
• Key findings with sources
• Pros & cons
• Competitor landscape
• Action recommendations' }}

nodes.name: Telegram — Send doc link
nodes.notes: Sends the Google Docs link back to the user in Telegram with a summary of what's inside.
